diagrams.description: Historiography examines how historians have written, interpreted, and conceptualised the past. This flowchart outlines its analytical logic: the goal is to understand the methods, theories, and assumptions that shape historical knowledge. It integrates core components—sources (primary, secondary, oral, visual), methods and criticism (source criticism, archival research, quantitative analysis, textual analysis), and major theoretical schools (empiricism, Marxist historiography, Annales, postcolonial, feminist, digital history). These are framed by key concepts (objectivity, bias, periodisation, narrative, memory) and drive the process of historical writing: research → interpretation → narrative construction → historiographical critique → ongoing revision. The resulting insights reveal how history is produced, contested, and re‑interpreted across cultures and periods.
